_comment: Onshape drawing export config. See drawing-export-spec.md and CLAUDE.md.

77ccfe85426b6dca10c64431.what: Top-level robot assembly. Referenced by chassis V4/V13 auto-versions.
77ccfe85426b6dca10c64431.why_excluded: No drawings expected -- it consumes subsystems rather than detailing parts. Scanning it costs ~138-276 calls/cycle for nothing: it references all 5 subsystem docs, so every subsystem version triggers a reference refresh here, meaning its modifiedAt changes constantly. It would clear Stage 0 on nearly every run and burn a Stage 1 versions call, only to be rejected as purpose!=0 auto-versions or return 0 drawings.
77ccfe85426b6dca10c64431.when_to_add: If drawings are ever authored here, add it to `documents` with its subsystem number. Nothing else changes -- the cascade handles it.

output._paths: Relative to this config file, so a fresh clone works with no edits. When the tool lives at <repo>/tools/drawing-export/, repo_path '../..' IS the repo root. Absolute paths also work if you prefer.
output._no_version_suffix: git history IS the version history; issue links are commit-pinned blob URLs

subsystems._comment: Subsystem number -> human name, shown in issue comments. Not everyone knows the numbers. Costs no API call -- it's a local lookup. If a number is missing here, the name falls back to documents[].key, then to the bare number.

users._comment: Onshape user id -> GitHub login, for attributing drawings in issue comments. Run `py reconcile_users.py` to populate. Attribution comes from the VERSION creator (free), not the drawing author -- see store.py.
users._why_manual: Auto-matching mostly fails: 4 of 7 GitHub accounts on this repo have no `name` set, so there is nothing to match an Onshape display name against. Exact matches are applied automatically; everything else needs a human once.

_unresolved_policy_options.none: Post the comment with no @mention. Safe default -- never mentions the wrong person.
_unresolved_policy_options.subsystem_owner: @mention the owner of the drawing's subsystem (see subsystem_owners).

subsystem_owners._comment: Optional. Used only when unresolved_policy = subsystem_owner.

suggestions._comment: Written by reconcile_users.py for review. Move entries into `map` once confirmed, or delete them. NEVER auto-applied -- a wrong @mention pings the wrong student.

budget._note: Educator = 2500/YEAR shared company-wide. Reset date UNRESOLVED (spec 6). Assume the LATER reading (~Feb 19 2027). Never spend against an assumed reset.

project._comment: GitHub Project (v2) to file drawings into. Run project_setup.py --project N --apply once per season first.
project._number_note: CAD 2026 Rebuilt-B. Sandbox testing uses 19 via config.sandbox.json.
project._enabled_note: OFF until the Subsystem options on #18 match config.subsystems -- see project_setup.py.

_not_automated.Component Type (part studios only): FDM vs CNC is a fabrication decision. Drawings get DWG/ASM automatically.

_component_type.DWG: set automatically: a drawing of a PART
_component_type.ASM: set automatically: a drawing of a SUBASSEMBLY (the A prefix in the part number)
_component_type.FDM: human: 3D printed. Nothing in Onshape distinguishes printer-bound from mill-bound.
